title: São Paulo archipelago serves as a breeding ground for endangered sharks
excerpt: The critically endangered sand tiger shark (Carcharias taurus) appears to have found a refuge off the northern coast of the state of São Paulo, Brazil, for mating and reproduction. This was revealed in a study conducted by scientists and divers in the Alcatrazes Archipelago, a protected marine area located 35 kilometers (22 miles) off the coast of São Sebastião.
content_html:
The critically endangered sand tiger shark (Carcharias taurus) appears to have found a refuge off the northern coast of the state of São Paulo, Brazil, for mating and reproduction. This was revealed in a study conducted by scientists and divers in the Alcatrazes Archipelago, a protected marine area located 35 kilometers (22 miles) off the coast of São Sebastião.

The results were published in the Journal of Fish Biology by researchers from the Federal University of São Paulo (UNIFESP), São Paulo State University (UNESP) in São Vicente, and the Fisheries Institute of the São Paulo Agency for Agribusiness Technology (IP/APTA).

The researchers observed a female with recent mating marks in the summer and a pregnant female in the winter. Previous studies had reported that sand tiger sharks mated in Argentina, Uruguay and southern Brazil, then migrated to warmer waters off the southeastern Brazilian coast to gestate and give birth.

"We've shown that they're here not only in the winter as previously thought, but also in the summer, completing their entire reproductive cycle in Brazilian waters," says Ana Clara Athayde, the study's first author.

The observations were made at the Alcatrazes Archipelago Wildlife Refuge and underscore the importance of protected marine areas for the species and for ecosystem services such as providing food for fishing communities. The protected area, established in 2016, is managed by the Chico Mendes Institute for Biodiversity Conservation (ICMBio), which is affiliated with the Ministry of the Environment and Climate Change.

"There are historical records of sand tiger sharks being caught as bycatch off the São Paulo coast, but there were no scientific records until the record we compiled in Alcatrazes in the winter of 2022," says Fábio Motta, a professor at IMar-UNIFESP.

Most of the records were obtained using BRUVs (baited remote underwater video systems). In this technique, a mount with two cameras and an arm with bait is kept submerged for about an hour. After images are captured at various locations, they are analyzed to identify the species recorded by the equipment.

Citizen science

For this study, BRUVs were deployed 315 times at 38 locations throughout the archipelago at depths ranging from 2 to 50 meters (7 to 164 feet). Sampling was conducted in winter and summer from 2022 to 2025 as part of the Mar de Alcatrazes program, which is dedicated to monitoring the archipelago's biodiversity.

The sharks were counted and measured using the images. In addition to the researchers' records, the study relied on recreational divers' contributions as part of a citizen science initiative. Guilherme Bertuzo, a biologist, dive guide and author of the article, managed to film nine sand tiger sharks in the summer of 2024 at depths ranging from 5 to 10 meters (16 to 33 feet).

"Sand tiger sharks have a very distinctive morphology, with fin size and position that set them apart from other coastal species in the southwestern Atlantic. That's why we were able to identify them even when visibility wasn't ideal," Motta reports.

The researchers also identified the sex and stage of maturity (juvenile or adult) of the sharks, as well as their distinctive body markings, whenever possible. Pregnancy was inferred based on the distension of the ventral region of the females' bodies.

Additionally, a method published shortly before in the United States allowed the researchers to estimate when the females had mated, based on bite marks left by males during copulation, as well as the gestation period.

"By observing the healing of those wounds in captive females and comparing them with those in the wild based on citizen science records, those researchers were able to estimate when copulation occurred and consequently, the gestation period. Using this method, we showed that females on Alcatrazes are copulating," says Athayde.

Critically endangered

The sand tiger shark has a highly specialized and unique reproductive mode. This species was the first in which intrauterine cannibalism was described. In this phenomenon, the pups feed on the eggs—sometimes already fertilized—and even on their younger siblings while still in the mother's womb.

As a result, females typically give birth to only two pups per pregnancy, which usually lasts 9 to 12 months and can occur every two years. The pups are born large, measuring about 90 centimeters (35 inches). Low fertility is one vulnerability of this and other shark species, which are unable to withstand overfishing and other impacts, such as marine pollution and habitat loss.

According to the researchers, the Alcatrazes Archipelago and other nearby protected marine areas, such as Santos Reef and Queimada Grande Island, can form an important ecological corridor for conservation because the sand tiger shark is a migratory species.

In 2025, the Alcatrazes Archipelago was designated an Important Shark and Ray Area (ISRA) as part of a program by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) that identified such areas worldwide.

"Our findings underscore the importance of this particular conservation area, as well as other areas where fishing is regulated, for protecting coastal sharks. Furthermore, by revealing the spatial and temporal dynamics of the species, the study can inform conservation strategies," Motta concludes.

Publication details

Ana Clara S. Athayde et al, Subtropical marine reserve as key habitats for the critically endangered sand tiger shark ( Carcharias taurus ) in the Southwestern Atlantic, Journal of Fish Biology (2026). DOI: 10.1111/jfb.70479

Who's behind this story?

Robert Egan

Bachelor's in mathematical biology, Master's in creative writing. Well-traveled with unique perspectives on science and language. Full profile →

Citation: São Paulo archipelago serves as a breeding ground for endangered sharks (2026, July 21) retrieved 22 July 2026 from https://phys.org/news/2026-07-paulo-archipelago-ground-endangered-sharks.html

sentences.en: Sand tiger shark (Carcharias taurus) in the Alcatrazes Archipelago.
sentences.zh: 恶魔岛群岛的沙虎鲨（Carcharias taurus）。

sentences.en: This protected marine area is home to these sharks throughout their entire reproductive cycle.
sentences.zh: 这个受保护的海洋区域是这些鲨鱼整个繁殖周期的家园。

sentences.en: Credit: Octávio Campos Salles
sentences.zh: 图片来源：奥克塔维奥·坎波斯·萨勒斯

blocks.html_en: The critically endangered sand tiger shark (Carcharias taurus) appears to have found a refuge off the northern coast of the state of São Paulo, Brazil, for mating and reproduction. This was revealed in a study conducted by scientists and divers in the Alcatrazes Archipelago, a protected marine area located 35 kilometers (22 miles) off the coast of São Sebastião.

sentences.en: The critically endangered sand tiger shark (Carcharias taurus) appears to have found a refuge off the northern coast of the state of São Paulo, Brazil, for mating and reproduction.
sentences.zh: 极度濒临灭绝的沙虎鲨（Carcharias taurus）似乎在巴西圣保罗州北海岸附近找到了避难所，用于交配和繁殖。

sentences.en: This was revealed in a study conducted by scientists and divers in the Alcatrazes Archipelago, a protected marine area located 35 kilometers (22 miles) off the coast of São Sebastião.
sentences.zh: 科学家和潜水员在恶魔岛群岛进行的一项研究揭示了这一点，恶魔岛群岛是一个海洋保护区，距离圣塞巴斯蒂昂海岸 35 公里（22 英里）。

blocks.html_en: The results were published in the Journal of Fish Biology by researchers from the Federal University of São Paulo (UNIFESP), São Paulo State University (UNESP) in São Vicente, and the Fisheries Institute of the São Paulo Agency for Agribusiness Technology (IP/APTA).

sentences.en: The results were published in the Journal of Fish Biology by researchers from the Federal University of São Paulo (UNIFESP), São Paulo State University (UNESP) in São Vicente, and the Fisheries Institute of the São Paulo Agency for Agribusiness Technology (IP/APTA).
sentences.zh: 来自圣保罗联邦大学 (UNIFESP)、圣保罗州立大学 (UNESP) 和圣保罗农业企业技术局渔业研究所 (IP/APTA) 的研究人员将研究结果发表在《鱼类生物学杂志》上。

blocks.html_en: The researchers observed a female with recent mating marks in the summer and a pregnant female in the winter. Previous studies had reported that sand tiger sharks mated in Argentina, Uruguay and southern Brazil, then migrated to warmer waters off the southeastern Brazilian coast to gestate and give birth.

sentences.en: The researchers observed a female with recent mating marks in the summer and a pregnant female in the winter.
sentences.zh: 研究人员在夏天观察了一只最近有交配痕迹的雌性，在冬天观察了一只怀孕的雌性。

sentences.en: Previous studies had reported that sand tiger sharks mated in Argentina, Uruguay and southern Brazil, then migrated to warmer waters off the southeastern Brazilian coast to gestate and give birth.
sentences.zh: 此前的研究报告称，沙虎鲨在阿根廷、乌拉圭和巴西南部交配，然后迁徙到巴西东南部海岸较温暖的水域妊娠和分娩。

blocks.html_en: "We've shown that they're here not only in the winter as previously thought, but also in the summer, completing their entire reproductive cycle in Brazilian waters," says Ana Clara Athayde, the study's first author.

sentences.en: "We've shown that they're here not only in the winter as previously thought, but also in the summer, completing their entire reproductive cycle in Brazilian waters," says Ana Clara Athayde, the study's first author.
sentences.zh: 该研究的第一作者安娜·克拉拉·阿泰德 (Ana Clara Athayde) 表示：“我们已经证明，它们不仅像以前认为的那样在冬天出现，而且在夏天也在这里，完成它们在巴西水域的整个繁殖周期。”

blocks.html_en: The observations were made at the Alcatrazes Archipelago Wildlife Refuge and underscore the importance of protected marine areas for the species and for ecosystem services such as providing food for fishing communities. The protected area, established in 2016, is managed by the Chico Mendes Institute for Biodiversity Conservation (ICMBio), which is affiliated with the Ministry of the Environment and Climate Change.

sentences.en: The observations were made at the Alcatrazes Archipelago Wildlife Refuge and underscore the importance of protected marine areas for the species and for ecosystem services such as providing food for fishing communities.
sentences.zh: 这些观察是在阿尔卡特拉泽斯群岛野生动物保护区进行的，强调了海洋保护区对于物种和生态系统服务（例如为渔业社区提供食物）的重要性。

sentences.en: The protected area, established in 2016, is managed by the Chico Mendes Institute for Biodiversity Conservation (ICMBio), which is affiliated with the Ministry of the Environment and Climate Change.
sentences.zh: 该保护区成立于 2016 年，由隶属于环境和气候变化部的奇科门德斯生物多样性保护研究所 (ICMBio) 管理。

blocks.html_en: "There are historical records of sand tiger sharks being caught as bycatch off the São Paulo coast, but there were no scientific records until the record we compiled in Alcatrazes in the winter of 2022," says Fábio Motta, a professor at IMar-UNIFESP.

sentences.en: "There are historical records of sand tiger sharks being caught as bycatch off the São Paulo coast, but there were no scientific records until the record we compiled in Alcatrazes in the winter of 2022," says Fábio Motta, a professor at IMar-UNIFESP.
sentences.zh: IMar-UNIFESP 教授法比奥·莫塔 (Fábio Motta) 表示：“历史记录显示，沙虎鲨在圣保罗海岸被作为副渔获物捕获，但直到 2022 年冬天我们在阿尔卡特拉泽斯编制了记录之前，才出现了科学记录。”

blocks.html_en: Most of the records were obtained using BRUVs (baited remote underwater video systems). In this technique, a mount with two cameras and an arm with bait is kept submerged for about an hour. After images are captured at various locations, they are analyzed to identify the species recorded by the equipment.

sentences.en: Most of the records were obtained using BRUVs (baited remote underwater video systems).
sentences.zh: 大多数记录是使用 BRUV（诱饵远程水下视频系统）获得的。

sentences.en: In this technique, a mount with two cameras and an arm with bait is kept submerged for about an hour.
sentences.zh: 在这种技术中，带有两个摄像机的支架和带有诱饵的手臂被浸入水中大约一个小时。

sentences.en: After images are captured at various locations, they are analyzed to identify the species recorded by the equipment.
sentences.zh: 在不同地点捕获图像后，对图像进行分析以识别设备记录的物种。

sentences.zh: 公民科学

sentences.zh: 在这项研究中，BRUV 在整个群岛的 38 个地点部署了 315 次，深度范围为 2 至 50 米（7 至 164 英尺）。

sentences.en: Sampling was conducted in winter and summer from 2022 to 2025 as part of the Mar de Alcatrazes program, which is dedicated to monitoring the archipelago's biodiversity.
sentences.zh: 作为 Mar de Alcatrazes 计划的一部分，取样工作于 2022 年至 2025 年冬季和夏季进行，该计划致力于监测群岛的生物多样性。

sentences.zh: 使用这些图像对鲨鱼进行计数和测量。

sentences.en: In addition to the researchers' records, the study relied on recreational divers' contributions as part of a citizen science initiative.
sentences.zh: 除了研究人员的记录之外，该研究还依赖于休闲潜水员的贡献，作为公民科学倡议的一部分。

sentences.en: Guilherme Bertuzo, a biologist, dive guide and author of the article, managed to film nine sand tiger sharks in the summer of 2024 at depths ranging from 5 to 10 meters (16 to 33 feet).
sentences.zh: 生物学家、潜水向导兼文章作者 Guilherme Bertuzo 于 2024 年夏天在 5 至 10 米（16 至 33 英尺）的深度成功拍摄到了 9 条沙虎鲨。

blocks.html_en: "Sand tiger sharks have a very distinctive morphology, with fin size and position that set them apart from other coastal species in the southwestern Atlantic. That's why we were able to identify them even when visibility wasn't ideal," Motta reports.

sentences.en: "Sand tiger sharks have a very distinctive morphology, with fin size and position that set them apart from other coastal species in the southwestern Atlantic.
sentences.zh: “沙虎鲨具有非常独特的形态，鳍的大小和位置使它们与西南大西洋的其他沿海物种区分开来。

sentences.en: That's why we were able to identify them even when visibility wasn't ideal," Motta reports.
sentences.zh: 这就是为什么我们能够在能见度不理想的情况下识别它们，”莫塔报告道。

sentences.en: The researchers also identified the sex and stage of maturity (juvenile or adult) of the sharks, as well as their distinctive body markings, whenever possible.
sentences.zh: 研究人员还尽可能确定了鲨鱼的性别和成熟阶段（幼年或成年），以及它们独特的身体标记。

sentences.en: Pregnancy was inferred based on the distension of the ventral region of the females' bodies.
sentences.zh: 根据雌性身体腹侧区域的扩张来推断怀孕。

blocks.html_en: Additionally, a method published shortly before in the United States allowed the researchers to estimate when the females had mated, based on bite marks left by males during copulation, as well as the gestation period.

sentences.en: Additionally, a method published shortly before in the United States allowed the researchers to estimate when the females had mated, based on bite marks left by males during copulation, as well as the gestation period.
sentences.zh: 此外，美国不久前发表的一种方法允许研究人员根据雄性在交配期间留下的咬痕以及妊娠期来估计雌性交配的时间。

blocks.html_en: "By observing the healing of those wounds in captive females and comparing them with those in the wild based on citizen science records, those researchers were able to estimate when copulation occurred and consequently, the gestation period. Using this method, we showed that females on Alcatrazes are copulating," says Athayde.

sentences.en: "By observing the healing of those wounds in captive females and comparing them with those in the wild based on citizen science records, those researchers were able to estimate when copulation occurred and consequently, the gestation period.
sentences.zh: “通过观察圈养雌性伤口的愈合情况，并根据公民科学记录将其与野外的伤口进行比较，这些研究人员能够估计交配发生的时间，从而估计妊娠期。

sentences.en: Using this method, we showed that females on Alcatrazes are copulating," says Athayde.
sentences.zh: 使用这种方法，我们发现恶魔岛上的雌性正在交配，”Athayde 说。

blocks.html_en: Critically endangered

sentences.en: Critically endangered
sentences.zh: 极度濒危

blocks.html_en: The sand tiger shark has a highly specialized and unique reproductive mode. This species was the first in which intrauterine cannibalism was described. In this phenomenon, the pups feed on the eggs—sometimes already fertilized—and even on their younger siblings while still in the mother's womb.

sentences.en: The sand tiger shark has a highly specialized and unique reproductive mode.
sentences.zh: 沙虎鲨具有高度专业化且独特的繁殖方式。

sentences.en: This species was the first in which intrauterine cannibalism was described.
sentences.zh: 该物种是第一个被描述为宫内同类相食的物种。

sentences.en: In this phenomenon, the pups feed on the eggs—sometimes already fertilized—and even on their younger siblings while still in the mother's womb.
sentences.zh: 在这种现象中，幼崽以卵为食——有时已经受精——甚至还在母亲子宫里的时候就以它们的弟弟妹妹为食。

blocks.html_en: As a result, females typically give birth to only two pups per pregnancy, which usually lasts 9 to 12 months and can occur every two years. sentences.en: As a result, females typically give birth to only two pups per pregnancy, which usually lasts 9 to 12 months and can occur every two years.
sentences.zh: 因此，雌性每次怀孕通常只产两只幼崽，怀孕期通常持续 9 至 12 个月，每两年可能发生一次。

sentences.en: The pups are born large, measuring about 90 centimeters (35 inches).
sentences.zh: 幼崽出生时体型较大，身长约为 90 厘米（35 英寸）。

sentences.en: Low fertility is one vulnerability of this and other shark species, which are unable to withstand overfishing and other impacts, such as marine pollution and habitat loss.
sentences.zh: 低生育率是这种鲨鱼和其他鲨鱼物种的一个弱点，它们无法承受过度捕捞和其他影响，例如海洋污染和栖息地丧失。

blocks.html_en: According to the researchers, the Alcatrazes Archipelago and other nearby protected marine areas, such as Santos Reef and Queimada Grande Island, can form an important ecological corridor for conservation because the sand tiger shark is a migratory species.

sentences.en: According to the researchers, the Alcatrazes Archipelago and other nearby protected marine areas, such as Santos Reef and Queimada Grande Island, can form an important ecological corridor for conservation because the sand tiger shark is a migratory species.
sentences.zh: 研究人员表示，恶魔岛群岛和附近的其他海洋保护区，如桑托斯礁和凯马达岛，可以形成重要的生态走廊，因为沙虎鲨是一种迁徙物种。

blocks.html_en: In 2025, the Alcatrazes Archipelago was designated an Important Shark and Ray Area (ISRA) as part of a program by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) that identified such areas worldwide.

sentences.en: In 2025, the Alcatrazes Archipelago was designated an Important Shark and Ray Area (ISRA) as part of a program by the International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN) that identified such areas worldwide.
sentences.zh: 2025 年，恶魔岛群岛被指定为重要鲨鱼和鳐鱼区 (ISRA)，这是国际自然保护联盟 (IUCN) 在全球范围内确定此类区域的计划的一部分。

blocks.html_en: "Our findings underscore the importance of this particular conservation area, as well as other areas where fishing is regulated, for protecting coastal sharks. Furthermore, by revealing the spatial and temporal dynamics of the species, the study can inform conservation strategies," Motta concludes.

sentences.en: "Our findings underscore the importance of this particular conservation area, as well as other areas where fishing is regulated, for protecting coastal sharks.
sentences.zh: “我们的研究结果强调了这个特定保护区以及其他监管捕鱼的区域对于保护沿海鲨鱼的重要性。

sentences.en: Furthermore, by revealing the spatial and temporal dynamics of the species, the study can inform conservation strategies," Motta concludes.
sentences.zh: 此外，通过揭示物种的空间和时间动态，该研究可以为保护策略提供信息，”莫塔总结道。

content_html_zh:
极度濒临灭绝的沙虎鲨（Carcharias taurus）似乎在巴西圣保罗州北海岸附近找到了避难所，用于交配和繁殖。 科学家和潜水员在恶魔岛群岛进行的一项研究揭示了这一点，恶魔岛群岛是一个海洋保护区，距离圣塞巴斯蒂昂海岸 35 公里（22 英里）。

来自圣保罗联邦大学 (UNIFESP)、圣保罗州立大学 (UNESP) 和圣保罗农业企业技术局渔业研究所 (IP/APTA) 的研究人员将研究结果发表在《鱼类生物学杂志》上。

研究人员在夏天观察了一只最近有交配痕迹的雌性，在冬天观察了一只怀孕的雌性。 此前的研究报告称，沙虎鲨在阿根廷、乌拉圭和巴西南部交配，然后迁徙到巴西东南部海岸较温暖的水域妊娠和分娩。

该研究的第一作者安娜·克拉拉·阿泰德 (Ana Clara Athayde) 表示：“我们已经证明，它们不仅像以前认为的那样在冬天出现，而且在夏天也在这里，完成它们在巴西水域的整个繁殖周期。”

这些观察是在阿尔卡特拉泽斯群岛野生动物保护区进行的，强调了海洋保护区对于物种和生态系统服务（例如为渔业社区提供食物）的重要性。 该保护区成立于 2016 年，由隶属于环境和气候变化部的奇科门德斯生物多样性保护研究所 (ICMBio) 管理。

IMar-UNIFESP 教授法比奥·莫塔 (Fábio Motta) 表示：“历史记录显示，沙虎鲨在圣保罗海岸被作为副渔获物捕获，但直到 2022 年冬天我们在阿尔卡特拉泽斯编制了记录之前，才出现了科学记录。”

大多数记录是使用 BRUV（诱饵远程水下视频系统）获得的。 在这种技术中，带有两个摄像机的支架和带有诱饵的手臂被浸入水中大约一个小时。 在不同地点捕获图像后，对图像进行分析以识别设备记录的物种。

公民科学

在这项研究中，BRUV 在整个群岛的 38 个地点部署了 315 次，深度范围为 2 至 50 米（7 至 164 英尺）。 作为 Mar de Alcatrazes 计划的一部分，取样工作于 2022 年至 2025 年冬季和夏季进行，该计划致力于监测群岛的生物多样性。

使用这些图像对鲨鱼进行计数和测量。 除了研究人员的记录之外，该研究还依赖于休闲潜水员的贡献，作为公民科学倡议的一部分。 生物学家、潜水向导兼文章作者 Guilherme Bertuzo 于 2024 年夏天在 5 至 10 米（16 至 33 英尺）的深度成功拍摄到了 9 条沙虎鲨。

“沙虎鲨具有非常独特的形态，鳍的大小和位置使它们与西南大西洋的其他沿海物种区分开来。 这就是为什么我们能够在能见度不理想的情况下识别它们，”莫塔报告道。

研究人员还尽可能确定了鲨鱼的性别和成熟阶段（幼年或成年），以及它们独特的身体标记。 根据雌性身体腹侧区域的扩张来推断怀孕。

此外，美国不久前发表的一种方法允许研究人员根据雄性在交配期间留下的咬痕以及妊娠期来估计雌性交配的时间。

“通过观察圈养雌性伤口的愈合情况，并根据公民科学记录将其与野外的伤口进行比较，这些研究人员能够估计交配发生的时间，从而估计妊娠期。 使用这种方法，我们发现恶魔岛上的雌性正在交配，”Athayde 说。

极度濒危

沙虎鲨具有高度专业化且独特的繁殖方式。 该物种是第一个被描述为宫内同类相食的物种。 在这种现象中，幼崽以卵为食——有时已经受精——甚至还在母亲子宫里的时候就以它们的弟弟妹妹为食。

因此，雌性每次怀孕通常只产两只幼崽，怀孕期通常持续 9 至 12 个月，每两年可能发生一次。 幼崽出生时体型较大，身长约为 90 厘米（35 英寸）。 低生育率是这种鲨鱼和其他鲨鱼物种的一个弱点，它们无法承受过度捕捞和其他影响，例如海洋污染和栖息地丧失。

研究人员表示，恶魔岛群岛和附近的其他海洋保护区，如桑托斯礁和凯马达岛，可以形成重要的生态走廊，因为沙虎鲨是一种迁徙物种。

2025 年，恶魔岛群岛被指定为重要鲨鱼和鳐鱼区 (ISRA)，这是国际自然保护联盟 (IUCN) 在全球范围内确定此类区域的计划的一部分。

“我们的研究结果强调了这个特定保护区以及其他监管捕鱼的区域对于保护沿海鲨鱼的重要性。 此外，通过揭示物种的空间和时间动态，该研究可以为保护策略提供信息，”莫塔总结道。

出版详情

安娜·克拉拉·S。 Athayde 等人，亚热带海洋保护区是西南大西洋极度濒危沙虎鲨（Carcharias taurus）的主要栖息地，《鱼类生物学杂志》（2026 年）。 DOI：10.1111/jfb.70479

谁是这个故事的幕后黑手？

罗伯特·伊根

数学生物学学士学位，创意写作硕士学位。 游历广泛，对科学和语言有独特的观点。 完整档案 →
